description:
This map displays the detailed land use and land cover (LULC) of the Bruxelles/Brussel/Leuven Functional Urban Area (FUA), identified as BE001L3, for the reference year 2021. The map is part of the Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) Urban Atlas product, generated by CLS.

The main map highlights the BE001L3 FUA, outlined in red, against a satellite imagery background. Surrounding FUAs are labelled with their codes and names: BE003L2 Gent, BE007L2 Antwerpen, BE012L1 Mechelen, BE005L2 Liège, BE007L2 Namur, BE004L2 Charleroi, BE014L1 La Louvière, BE009L1 Mons. A smaller inset map shows Belgium's location within Europe, and another inset provides a regional context within Belgium, indicating the FUA of Brussels with a red star.

The legend classifies land cover into 30 categories with specific colour codes, which can be grouped into Artificial surfaces, Green urban areas, Agricultural and Semi-Natural Areas, and Water. Key categories include:
*   **Artificial surfaces:**
    *   11100 Continuous urban fabric (Surface Level (S.L.): > 80%) - Dark red
    *   11210 Discontinuous dense urban fabric (S.L.: 50% - 80%) - Red
    *   11220 Discontinuous medium density urban fabric (S.L.: 30% - 50%) - Lighter red
    *   11230 Discontinuous low density urban fabric (S.L.: 10% - 30%) - Pink
    *   11240 Discontinuous very low density urban fabric (S.L.: < 10%) - Light pink
    *   11300 Isolated structures - Purple
    *   12100 Industrial, commercial, public, military and private units - Dark purple/brown
    *   12210 Fast transport roads and associated land - Brown
    *   12220 Other roads and associated land - Lighter brown
    *   12230 Railways and associated land - Darker brown
    *   12300 Port areas - Grey-brown
    *   12400 Airports - Light grey-brown
    *   13100 Mineral extraction and dump sites - Light purple
    *   13300 Construction sites - Darker grey-blue
    *   13400 Land without current use - Grey-blue
*   **Green Urban Areas:**
    *   14110 Green urban areas (Public spaces) - Bright green
    *   14120 Green urban areas (Private spaces) - Lighter bright green
    *   14130 Green urban areas (Leisure, sports, amenities) - Dark green
    *   14200 Sports and leisure facilities - Yellow-green
*   **Agricultural and Semi-Natural Areas:**
    *   21000 Arable land (annual crops) - Yellow
    *   22000 Permanent crops - Yellow-orange
    *   23000 Pastures - Light green
    *   24000 Complex and mixed cultivation patterns - Green-yellow
    *   31000 Forests - Darker green
    *   32000 Herbaceous vegetation associations - Light blue-green
    *   33000 Open spaces with little or no vegetation - Yellow-grey
    *   40000 Wetlands - Light blue
*   **Water:**
    *   50000 Water - Dark blue

The spatial pattern shows a concentrated urban core (Brussels) with high imperviousness, dominated by continuous and discontinuous urban fabric, surrounded by a mosaic of agricultural fields (yellow), forests (dark green), and smaller settlements. Green urban areas are interspersed within the urban fabric.

The map information indicates that the data is derived from Very High Resolution (VHR) satellite imagery, processed using semi-automatic Sentinel-2 time series classification and visual interpretation. The map date is 05 August 2021. The scale is 1:392,897 for an A3 format, with a scale bar showing 0 to 10 km. The projection is EPSG: 3035 - ETRS89-extended / LAEA Europe.
